title: artemisia tangutica var. tomentosa
intro: Artemisia tangutica var. tomentosa, also known as Mongolian wormwood, is a type of flowering plant in the aster family that is native to the mountainous regions of China and Mongolia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mongolian wormwood include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating pain, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. In herbal preparations, Mongolian wormwood is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
